title: Sovereign inference twin — OpenAI-wire-compatible REST twin routing to optimal local models via declared policy spec with full inference observability
description: The OpenAI API has a published OpenAPI spec. The twin speaks it: POST /v1/chat/completions, GET /v1/models, POST /v1/embeddings. Any client built against the OpenAI API (LangChain, LlamaIndex, aibuildout, custom agents) works against the twin without code changes. Instead of placeholder responses, the twin routes each request to a local model backend (llama.cpp, ollama, MLX, vLLM) and returns the model's actual output in OpenAI response format. Routing layer: twin inspects each request and routes to optimal local model based on a declared policy spec — match on model glob, temperature range, tool presence, context length, fallback. Policy spec is a schema artifact enforced by the twin; client never sees the routing. Observability: every inference call is observable through the twin — latency, token counts, model selected, estimated local compute cost, cache hit rate; emits as structured metrics alongside the response; aibuildout pipeline gets complete inference audit trail without client instrumentation. Cost arbitrage: twin tracks which requests required large model capability and which were satisfied by small models; this dataset is training signal for improving the routing policy — routing model improves its own training data by observing outcomes of its own routing decisions. Contract enforcement: OpenAI spec declares valid parameters; twin rejects invalid requests before they reach any model backend — malformed tool definitions, out-of-range temperature, contradictory stream settings fail fast at protocol layer not deep in model tokenization pipeline. Prerequisite: REST twin stable, local model adapter layer (HTTP to llama.cpp/ollama protocol). Documented in docs/PLAN_TWINNING_ALIEN_DIRECTIONS.md section 9.

title: REST child singleton lookup refuses terminal ID aliases when resource has primary key
description:
Autonomous REST e2e campaign finding from the official GitHub REST API OpenAPI 3.x description, reduced to a repository route subset.

Spec URL:
https://raw.githubusercontent.com/github/rest-api-description/main/descriptions/api.github.com/api.github.com.yaml

Local repro setup:
```bash
curl -L --fail https://raw.githubusercontent.com/github/rest-api-description/main/descriptions/api.github.com/api.github.com.yaml -o /tmp/github_api.yaml
cargo run -- rest --spec /tmp/github_repos_subset.yaml --serve --port 18141
python3 /tmp/github_repos_subset_client.py http://127.0.0.1:18141
```

Representative repro:
```bash
curl -i http://127.0.0.1:18141/repos/octocat/Hello-World/issues/1
curl -i http://127.0.0.1:18141/repos/octocat/Hello-World/labels/bug
curl -i http://127.0.0.1:18141/repos/octocat/Hello-World/keys/1
```

Actual response pattern:
```http
HTTP/1.1 501 Not Implemented
content-type: application/json

{"code":"unsupported_shape","detail":"resource `issues` has no path parameter matching primary key column `id`"}
```

Expected response/output:
A child singleton path with a terminal resource identifier should execute as a lookup scoped by the parent params. Empty state may return structured 404, but it should not refuse the route just because the terminal path parameter is named `issue_number`, `name`, or `key_id` while the component schema primary key column is `id`.

Campaign client excerpt:
```text
FAIL operation: GET /repos/octocat/Hello-World/issues/1: status=501, expected=200, body={'code': 'unsupported_shape', 'detail': 'resource `issues` has no path parameter matching primary key column `id`'}
FAIL operation: GET /repos/octocat/Hello-World/labels/bug: status=501, expected=200, body={'code': 'unsupported_shape', 'detail': 'resource `labels` has no path parameter matching primary key column `id`'}
FAIL operation: GET /repos/octocat/Hello-World/keys/1: status=501, expected=200, body={'code': 'unsupported_shape', 'detail': 'resource `deploy-keys` has no path parameter matching primary key column `id`'}
```

Root cause hypothesis:
`src/protocol/rest/normalize.rs::pk_predicate()` requires a path parameter matching the primary-key column name when a resource has a declared primary key. The fallback `single_path_value(path_params)` around `src/protocol/rest/normalize.rs:755-762` only helps routes with exactly one path parameter. Multi-parameter child paths such as `/repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues/{issue_number}` have parent params plus a terminal resource param, so the terminal alias is rejected before scoped lookup can be built. A fix likely needs to prefer an exact PK-column match, then fall back to the terminal path parameter for child singleton/update/delete routes while still adding parent scope comparisons where declared columns match.

title: Implement live verify artifact intake and batch-only rule refusal

title: Add shared live-twin launch and teardown utilities for canary tests
description:
Context:
Every compatibility canary needs the same process lifecycle plumbing to boot a twin, wait for readiness, and tear it down cleanly. This issue isolates that shared harness layer.

Deliverables:
- shared helpers to launch the twin under test
- readiness and teardown handling for canary runs
- common tempdir and port allocation behavior for compatibility tests
acceptance_criteria:
1. Canary tests can start and stop a twin through one shared helper surface.
2. Readiness and teardown failures produce actionable test failures rather than leaked processes.
3. New canaries can reuse this layer without reimplementing process plumbing.
notes:
Target files: tests/canaries/support/** once introduced.
Reservation guidance: own process harnessing only; do not encode client-specific assertions here.

title: REST version-scoped validation overlays for v2/v3 differences
description: Represent version-specific REST validation rules so v2 and v3 twins can diverge where the OpenAPI document is shared but prose specifies different behavior. OpenFIGI MappingJob prose states that for V3 securityType2 is required for BASE_TICKER or ID_EXCH_SYMBOL; expiration is required when securityType2 is Option or Warrant; maturity is required when securityType2 is Pool.
design: Add a generic overlay mechanism tied to selected server variables or x-twinning-style extension data. Do not hard-code OpenFIGI in core runtime. If the published schema lacks machine-readable rules, encode them in checked-in fixture/overlay data used by OpenFIGI canaries. Return validation_failed with clear field/rule details.
acceptance_criteria: Tests show the same MappingJob can be accepted by v2 and rejected by v3 when the v3 overlay rule applies; valid v3 requests pass; overlays are opt-in and do not affect unrelated specs or unselected legacy mode.
notes: Depends on B04, B08, and B11. Touch likely files: crates/twinning-rest/src/{xext,spec,normalize,routes}.rs and OpenFIGI fixtures.

title: Contract dependency linter — static and dynamic detection of client reliance on undocumented API behavior
description: Build a standalone CLI tool: cdl lint --spec stripe.yaml --client-src ./src --client-tests ./tests. Two modes: Static mode reads client source code and the OpenAPI spec, finds every place the client accesses a response field/header/status code, cross-references against the spec — fields accessed but not declared are flagged as UNDECLARED_DEPENDENCY, no API calls needed. Dynamic mode runs the client test suite against a twin, intercepts every response the twin sends, tracks which response fields the client code actually accesses at runtime, compares to the spec's declared response shape — fields accessed but not in spec are RUNTIME_UNDECLARED_DEPENDENCY bugs that will break silently at the next major API version. Combined output is a contract dependency surface map for the client codebase — input to API upgrade planning: before upgrading from Stripe v3 to v4 run cdl lint against the v4 spec to find which undocumented behaviors are going away. There is no other way to surface this class of fragility: cargo clippy and type checking cannot see it; integration tests against the real API pass precisely because the undocumented behavior is present; only a contract-faithful twin that refuses to honor undocumented behavior can reveal the gap. As a CI gate: new failures in twin mode that were not there before mean a code change just introduced a new contract dependency — catch it at PR time not at the next breaking change. CMD+RVL angle: cmdrvl-cli against metadata API twin; aibuildout against all four external API twins. Documented in docs/PLAN_TWINNING_REST_STRATEGY.md section 6.

title: REST GET /bank-accounts accepts invalid integer query params
description:
Autonomous REST e2e campaign finding from the public Billingo API v3 OpenAPI 3.0.0 spec.

Spec URL:
https://api.apis.guru/v2/specs/billingo.hu/3.0.7/openapi.json

Local repro setup:
```bash
curl -L --fail https://api.apis.guru/v2/specs/billingo.hu/3.0.7/openapi.json -o /tmp/billingo_openapi.json
cargo run -- rest --spec /tmp/billingo_openapi.json --serve --port 18213
```

Exact curl repro for invalid integer query:
```bash
curl -i 'http://127.0.0.1:18213/bank-accounts?page=not-a-valid-integer' \
  -H 'X-API-KEY: twinning-test'
```

Actual response:
```http
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
content-type: application/json

{"total":0,"current_page":0,"last_page":0,"next_page_url":"","per_page":0,"prev_page_url":"","data":[]}
```

Expected response:
The spec declares `page` as an integer query parameter on `GET /bank-accounts`, so `page=not-a-valid-integer` should be rejected with a 400/422 validation response instead of silently returning a successful list.

Second repro for required query presence:
```bash
curl -i 'http://127.0.0.1:18213/currencies' \
  -H 'X-API-KEY: twinning-test'
```

Actual response:
```http
HTTP/1.1 404 Not Found
content-type: application/json

{"code":"not_found","detail":"resource not found"}
```

Expected response:
The spec declares required query parameters `from` and `to` for `GET /currencies`; missing them should be a query validation error before resource lookup.

Campaign client excerpt:
```text
FAIL GET /bank-accounts invalid query type rejected: status=200
FAIL GET /currencies missing required query rejected: status=404
FAIL GET /currencies invalid query type rejected: status=404
FAIL GET /document-blocks invalid query type rejected: status=200
FAIL GET /documents invalid query type rejected: status=200
FAIL GET /partners invalid query type rejected: status=200
FAIL GET /products invalid query type rejected: status=200
SUMMARY operations=31 assertions=85 failures=10
```

Root cause hypothesis:
`src/protocol/rest/routes.rs:206-210` stores only query parameter names in `RouteEntry::declared_query_params`; `declared_query_params()` at `src/protocol/rest/routes.rs:1347-1374` drops each parameter schema and `required` flag. Runtime validation in `src/protocol/rest/normalize.rs:460-473` only rejects undeclared query names. `query_predicate()` at `src/protocol/rest/normalize.rs:1239-1272` skips pagination params and declared non-column params without coercing them through their OpenAPI schema, while `query_limit()` at `src/protocol/rest/normalize.rs:1281-1298` only validates the literal `limit` parameter, not `page`, `per_page`, or enum query parameters.

comments.author: CopperBasin
comments.text: Fixed in this session. REST routes now preserve query parameter metadata including required flags, schema types, and enum values. Request normalization now rejects missing required query parameters plus invalid declared integer, number, boolean, and enum query values before read normalization can return data. Billingo rerun: query validation failures dropped from seven to zero; remaining failures belong to bd-3dt0 and bd-1w1a. Validation passed: cargo fmt --check; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test.

title: Incremental data model synthesis — emerge a new Postgres schema one twin-verified DDL increment at a time, with query workload and constraint satisfiability validation at each step
description: Postgres-wire companion to bd-1qol (incremental spec synthesis). The schema DDL is the artifact under construction; the Postgres twin is the grounding oracle at each increment. An AI agent does domain research (understands entities, relationships, cardinalities, access patterns) and proposes the next DDL increment: a new table, column, constraint, index, or foreign key. Before proposing the next increment it applies the DDL to the twin and validates it. What the twin answers that reading DDL cannot: (a) Empty INSERT test — after each increment, attempt to INSERT a single row into each table with only required fields; if INSERT fails, a constraint is unsatisfiable: a NOT NULL column has no DEFAULT and no obvious population path, or a circular FK makes it impossible to populate both tables simultaneously; surfaces unsatisfiable constraint designs before anything is built on top of them; (b) Query workload validation — alongside each schema increment, declare the access patterns this model must serve; twin the schema, run EXPLAIN on all declared queries; if a join produces a seq scan because an FK index is missing, the twin says so now, not when the table has 10M rows; (c) Constraint satisfiability — circular foreign keys, overlapping unique constraints, CHECK constraints that conflict; the twin attempts to satisfy all declared constraints simultaneously; constraint sets that are individually valid but collectively impossible fail at the twin not in production; (d) Migration path generation — each increment is a verified DDL delta; the full migration from schema v0 to vN is the ordered sequence of verified deltas; migrations are not written after the fact and hoped to be correct, they are the incremental design steps already proved correct by the twin at each step; (e) Query workload oracle — declare N queries the data model must serve efficiently upfront; run EXPLAIN on all of them after every increment; any increment that causes a plan regression on a declared query is flagged before it is locked in; the data model converges toward one provably capable of serving its declared workload; (f) Normalization lens — after each increment the agent checks for normalization issues (repeating groups, partial dependencies, transitive dependencies); not to mandate normal form but to make trade-offs explicit at design time when they are cheap to fix. Key distinction from migration-proof twin (bd-1nyq direction) and from existing migration proof work: this is about designing a schema from scratch with verification at each step, not about proving an existing migration is correct. CMD+RVL angle: designing new tables for the metadata database, the Cairn data model, any new data product. Instead of designing a schema on paper and discovering constraint problems during implementation, each design decision is verified before the next is made. Prerequisite: Postgres tournament twin v0 complete. Companion to bd-1qol (REST spec synthesis), bd-gwlk (SQL tournament), bd-2ndf (codebase audit), bd-2r0q (query log replay). Documented in docs/PLAN_TWINNING_ALIEN_DIRECTIONS.md section 14.

title: Implement sqlalchemy_core canary against the declared read subset

Deliverables:

acceptance_criteria:

3. Any metadata surface exercised by the canary is limited to bd-1ot; no broader reflection claim is implied.
notes:

Reservation guidance: keep ORM compatibility narrow and manifest-backed.

title: REST create/update validate against response resource instead of requestBody schema
description:
Autonomous REST e2e campaign finding from a public Todo CRUD OpenAPI spec.

Spec source repo:
https://github.com/mariusnn/openapi_todo_app

Spec URL:
https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mariusnn/openapi_todo_app/main/src/openapi.yaml

Local repro setup:
```bash
curl -L --fail https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mariusnn/openapi_todo_app/main/src/openapi.yaml -o /tmp/mariusnn_openapi_todo.yaml
cargo run -- rest --spec /tmp/mariusnn_openapi_todo.yaml --port 18081
```

Repro request 1: POST uses `NewTodo` requestBody where only `title` is required:
```bash
curl -i -X POST 'http://127.0.0.1:18081/todos' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"title":"Buy milk","description":"2% milk"}'
```

Actual response 1:
```http
HTTP/1.1 422 Unprocessable Entity
content-type: application/json

{"code":"missing_required_field","detail":"required field `completed` is missing on resource `todos`","field":"completed","resource":"todos"}
```

Expected response 1:
The request conforms to the operation requestBody schema `NewTodo` (`title` is the only required field), so the REST twin should accept it or produce a precise unsupported-server-generated-field/default refusal. It should not require `Todo.completed` from the response resource schema.

Repro request 2: PUT uses `UpdateTodo` requestBody where all fields are optional:
```bash
# seed a row first
curl -i -X POST 'http://127.0.0.1:18081/todos' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"id":"todo-1","title":"Buy eggs","completed":false}'

# update with the declared UpdateTodo schema
curl -i -X PUT 'http://127.0.0.1:18081/todos/todo-1' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"completed":true}'
```

Actual response 2:
```http
HTTP/1.1 422 Unprocessable Entity
content-type: application/json

{"code":"missing_required_field","detail":"required field `id` is missing on resource `todos`","field":"id","resource":"todos"}
```

Expected response 2:
`PUT /todos/{id}` declares requestBody schema `UpdateTodo`, which has no required fields. The twin should validate/coerce the body against `UpdateTodo` and update the existing row, or produce a precise unsupported full-replace-vs-operation-schema refusal. It should not require response schema field `Todo.id` in the JSON body when the id is already supplied as a path parameter.

Client used for the campaign:
`/tmp/mariusnn_openapi_todo_client.py` produced 2 failures: create with `NewTodo` body and update with `UpdateTodo` body. Full CRUD with a full `Todo` body otherwise works.

Root cause hypothesis:
`src/protocol/rest/routes.rs` `RouteEntry` stores `response_schema_ref` but no request body schema reference. `route_entry()` around lines 246-265 extracts only response metadata. Then `src/protocol/rest/normalize.rs` `normalize_insert()` and `normalize_update()` validate with `validate_required(resource, &object)` and `mutation_fields_from_object(resource, ...)` around lines 167-188 and 231-245, where `resource` is the route response/table resource (`Todo`). The normalizer therefore ignores operation-level `requestBody` schemas (`NewTodo`, `UpdateTodo`). A fix likely needs `RouteEntry.request_schema_ref`/request fields plus a mapping from request schema fields to the target table columns, with explicit handling for server-generated required response fields such as `id`.
close_reason: Completed: verified requestBody schemas drive REST mutation validation with precise synthesize-required-fields refusal

comments.author: zac
comments.text: Reopened: Nexmo External Accounts shows the same requestBody-vs-target validation class still fails for inline requestBody schemas. Repro: curl -i -X POST 'http://127.0.0.1:18137/messenger' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"external_id":"page-123","access_token":"token-abc"}' against https://api.apis.guru/v2/specs/nexmo.com/external-accounts/0.1.5/openapi.json. Actual: 422 missing_required_field api_key on resource messengeraccountresponses. Expected: validate against the inline requestBody schema where external_id and access_token are the required fields, then either accept/create or return the precise cannot-synthesize target fields refusal. Hypothesis: routes.rs request_schema_ref() only records -derived schemas; inline requestBody object schemas leave request_resource_name unset, so normalize.rs falls back to the response/effective resource.

comments.author: Zac
comments.text: Correction/detail for the Nexmo reopen note: the route layer records request schemas only when request_schema_ref() can derive a local component $ref (or array item $ref). Inline requestBody object schemas leave request_resource_name unset even though request_body_schema_present is true, so normalize.rs falls back to the response/effective resource.

title: REST normalizer masks route-refusal details behind undeclared-resource errors
description:
Autonomous REST e2e campaign finding from the USPTO Data Set API OpenAPI 3.0 spec.

Spec URL:
https://unpkg.com/@readme/oas-examples@5.19.2/3.0/yaml/uspto.yaml

Local repro setup:
```bash
curl -L --fail https://unpkg.com/@readme/oas-examples@5.19.2/3.0/yaml/uspto.yaml -o /tmp/uspto.yaml
cargo run -- rest --spec /tmp/uspto.yaml --port 18080
```

Startup warnings correctly classify the unsupported routes:
```text
REST route is outside the CRUD route subset in v0. Path: /
Nested resource paths not supported in REST twin v0. Path: /{dataset}/{version}/fields
Nested resource paths not supported in REST twin v0. Path: /{dataset}/{version}/records
```

Actual HTTP responses mask those route-refusal details as undeclared catalog resources:
```bash
curl -i http://127.0.0.1:18080/
curl -i http://127.0.0.1:18080/oa_citations/v1/fields
curl -i -X POST http://127.0.0.1:18080/oa_citations/v1/records \
  -H "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" \
  --data "criteria=*:*&start=0&rows=2"
```

Actual output:
```http
HTTP/1.1 501 Not Implemented
content-type: application/json

{"code":"unsupported_shape","detail":"resource `` is not declared in the REST catalog"}

HTTP/1.1 501 Not Implemented
content-type: application/json

{"code":"unsupported_shape","detail":"resource `dataset` is not declared in the REST catalog"}

HTTP/1.1 501 Not Implemented
content-type: application/json

{"code":"unsupported_shape","detail":"resource `dataset` is not declared in the REST catalog"}
```

Expected output:
The HTTP response should preserve the `RouteKind::Refusal` detail already computed by the route registry, for example `REST route is outside the CRUD route subset in v0. Path: /` for the root path and `Nested resource paths not supported in REST twin v0...` for the two USPTO nested paths. If the project later chooses to support these OpenAPI shapes, that can be a separate feature; this bug is about the current refusal envelope being misleading and less actionable than startup warnings.

Client used for the campaign:
`/tmp/uspto_client.py http://127.0.0.1:18080` produced 10 failures, all explained by this masked-refusal behavior for `/`, `/{dataset}/{version}/fields`, and `/{dataset}/{version}/records`.

Root cause hypothesis:
`src/protocol/rest/routes.rs:277-284` builds precise `RouteKind::Refusal` details, but `src/protocol/rest/normalize.rs:68-75` calls `resource_schema(catalog, &route.resource_name)?` before matching `route.kind`. For refusal routes whose first segment is empty or a path parameter name, resource lookup fails first and discards the route refusal. Move the `RouteKind::Refusal` arm ahead of resource lookup, or only fetch `ResourceSchema` for executable CRUD route kinds.

title: REST object-field array normalization and validation

Do not stringify arrays into Text.

title: REST PUT /groups/{group_id} validates Box update body against response fields
description:
Autonomous REST e2e campaign finding from the official Box Platform API OpenAPI 3.0.2 spec.

Spec URL:
https://raw.githubusercontent.com/box/box-openapi/main/openapi.json

Local repro setup:
```bash
curl -L --fail https://raw.githubusercontent.com/box/box-openapi/main/openapi.json -o /tmp/box_openapi.json
cargo run -- rest --spec /tmp/box_openapi.json --serve --port 18195
python3 /tmp/box_openapi_client.py http://127.0.0.1:18195 /tmp/box_openapi.json
```

Exact curl repro:
```bash
curl -i -X PUT 'http://127.0.0.1:18195/groups/123' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"name":"Customer Support"}'
```

Actual response:
```http
HTTP/1.1 422 Unprocessable Entity
content-type: application/json

{"code":"missing_required_field","detail":"required field `id` is missing on resource `group--fulls`","field":"id","resource":"group--fulls"}
```

Expected response/output:
The request body for `PUT /groups/{group_id}` is an inline object schema whose fields are all optional update fields (`name`, `provenance`, `description`, etc.). The path supplies the target id as `{group_id}`. The twin should either execute the update/return an empty-state 404, or produce a precise unsupported full-replace refusal. It should not reject the request because the response resource `Group--Full` has required server fields such as `id` or `type` that are not declared in the request body.

Campaign client excerpt:
```text
FAIL PUT /groups/{group_id} valid mutation body accepted or explicitly refused: status=422, body='{"code":"missing_required_field","detail":"required field `id` is missing on resource `group--fulls`","field":"id","resource":"group--fulls"}'
FAIL PUT /web_links/{web_link_id} valid mutation body accepted or explicitly refused: status=422, body='{"code":"missing_required_field","detail":"required field `id` is missing on resource `weblinks`"...}'
FAILURE_CLASSES
valid_mutation_rejected: 28
```

Root cause hypothesis:
This is a narrower remaining gap after `bd-s295`. `normalize_update()` in `src/protocol/rest/normalize.rs:310-335` chooses `BodyMode::FullReplace` when the effective resource and request resource are not distinct. For inline Box update bodies, the normalizer then materializes every target/response resource column in `mutation_fields_from_object()` (`src/protocol/rest/normalize.rs:1092-1099`), filling absent response-only fields with null. Kernel required-field validation then rejects missing `id`/`type` even though those fields are not part of the operation request schema and the path id is already available for the predicate.

title: bd-v1r.1: RoutingPolicy enum, RoutingConfig struct, and x-twinning extension parser
description:
## Parent epic
bd-3j4y — Adaptive routing policy engine

## Depends on
bd-2svi (bd-v1r.0) — RoutingConfig must be defined before topology struct can reference it

## Goal
Define RoutingPolicy enum, RoutingConfig struct, and parse the x-twinning OpenAPI extension.

## CRITICAL CODE REALITY: OpenApiDoc has no extensions field

The current OpenApiDoc struct in spec.rs only has: openapi, info, components, paths.
There is NO extensions field. The x-twinning extension CANNOT be accessed via spec.extensions.

Fix: add an extensions field to OpenApiDoc:
```rust
pub struct OpenApiDoc {
    pub openapi: Option<String>,
    pub info: Option<InfoObject>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub components: ComponentsObject,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub paths: HashMap<String, PathItem>,
    #[serde(default, flatten)]
    pub extensions: HashMap<String, serde_yaml::Value>,  // captures x-twinning and other extensions
}
```

OR: since build_rest_catalog() consumes OpenApiDoc, parse x-twinning FIRST (before calling
build_rest_catalog), then pass the parsed XTwinningExt into RestCatalog for later use.

Recommended approach: parse x-twinning before consumption, store Option<XTwinningExt> in RestCatalog.
Add to RestCatalog:
```rust
pub x_twinning: Option<XTwinningExt>,
```
And populate it in build_rest_catalog() before the document is consumed.

## Deliverables

### 1. RoutingPolicy enum — src/protocol/rest/policy.rs (new file)

```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub enum RoutingPolicy {
    FlatCrud,
    SchemaFirst,
    PrefixScoped,
    Auto,
}
```

Implement FromStr (for CLI parsing) and Display. Derive clap::ValueEnum for CLI integration.

Note: Nested is NOT a RoutingPolicy — it is a ResourceRelationship variant in the topology.

### 2. RoutingConfig struct

```rust
pub struct RoutingConfig {
    pub policy: RoutingPolicy,
    pub base_prefix: Option<String>,
}
impl Default for RoutingConfig {
    fn default() -> Self { Self { policy: RoutingPolicy::Auto, base_prefix: None } }
}
```

### 3. XTwinningExt struct and parser — src/protocol/rest/xext.rs (new file)

```rust
pub struct XTwinningExt {
    pub routing: Option<RoutingPolicy>,
    pub base_prefix: Option<String>,
    pub auth_mode: Option<String>,
}

pub fn parse_x_twinning(doc: &OpenApiDoc) -> Option<XTwinningExt>
```

Access via the extensions field once added to OpenApiDoc, or extract before consuming the document.

### 4. resolve_routing_config merge helper

```rust
pub fn resolve_routing_config(
    cli: Option<RoutingPolicy>,
    cli_prefix: Option<String>,
    ext: Option<&XTwinningExt>,
) -> RoutingConfig
```

Precedence: CLI > x-twinning > Auto default.

## Tests

- parse_x_twinning_full: round-trip a spec with all x-twinning fields set
- resolve_routing_config_cli_wins
- routing_policy_from_str_roundtrip: all 4 enum variants (NOT nested)

## Acceptance

cargo test -p twinning rest::policy and rest::xext pass.
x-twinning extension is correctly read from OpenApiDoc (not silently ignored).

title: Universal protocol bridge — route any client protocol through twin kernel to any backend protocol
description: All twin types (pgwire, HTTP+REST, MCP/JSON-RPC, VSAM, IMS) map internally to the same kernel operation model: read/write/scan/upsert/delete against a keyed in-memory store. Wire the output of one twin adapter to the input of another. Use case A: VSAM twin receives COBOL file I/O, translates to kernel operations, routes to Postgres twin that answers via SQL — COBOL program reads a VSAM file; data lives in Postgres; no COBOL code changes. Use case B: SQLAlchemy ORM speaks pgwire to Postgres twin; twin translates SQL SELECT to REST API calls against an OpenAPI-spec upstream — ORM thinks it is querying a database; upstream is a microservice. The bridge is configuration not code: declare source schema (VSAM copybook), declare target schema (OpenAPI spec), declare field mapping; twin handles protocol translation and constraint enforcement on both sides simultaneously. Key insight: every CMD+RVL integration point is a potential protocol bridge — aibuildout pipeline, metadata API, cmdrvl-cli all speak different protocol dialects against the same underlying data. Prerequisite: VSAM twin and Postgres tournament twin both stable. Documented in docs/PLAN_TWINNING_ALIEN_DIRECTIONS.md section 2.

description:
Context:
The twinning boundary is now explicit. twinning does not define a second constraint language, does not shell out to batch verify in live mode, and does not collapse constraint results into its own pseudo-score. It executes verify through the embedded surface and reports twin-native metrics alongside attached verify outputs.

Deliverables:
- consume compiled verify.constraint.v1 artifacts through the twinning runtime surface
- execute verify through the embedded verify library path over materialized twin state
- attach verify results in a report shape that keeps verify semantics intact

- refuse batch-only verify rules explicitly in live twinning mode

2. Live twinning executes verify through the embedded library surface, not export-and-run batch invocation.
3. Twinning reports raw twin-native metrics and attached verify outputs without inventing a merged pseudo-score.
4. Batch-only verify rules are explicitly refused in live twinning mode.
5. The twin report carries the explicit v0 report contract, including run metadata when --run is used and omission of verify sections when no verify artifact is provided.
6. Benchmark scoring and assess policy remain outside twinning.

Reservation guidance: keep verify execution and attachment logic isolated from benchmark/assess concerns and align the report shape with the explicit v0 contract, including child-run metadata.

title: REST GET /api/v4/jobs/{id}/sbom_scans/{sbom_digest} requires non-template sbom_scan_id param
description:
Autonomous REST e2e campaign finding from the official GitLab OpenAPI 3.0.0 spec.

Spec URL:
https://gitlab.com/gitlab-org/gitlab/-/raw/master/doc/api/openapi/openapi_v3.yaml

Local repro setup:
```bash
curl -L --fail https://gitlab.com/gitlab-org/gitlab/-/raw/master/doc/api/openapi/openapi_v3.yaml -o /tmp/gitlab_openapi_v3.yaml
cargo run -- rest --spec /tmp/gitlab_openapi_v3.yaml --serve --port 18251
```

Representative repro:
```bash
curl -i 'http://127.0.0.1:18251/api/v4/jobs/12/sbom_scans/digest-abc'
```

Actual response:
```http
HTTP/1.1 422 Unprocessable Entity
content-type: application/json

{"code":"missing_required_field","detail":"required field `sbom_scan_id` is missing on resource `path`","field":"sbom_scan_id","resource":"path"}
```

Expected response/output:
The OpenAPI path template is `/api/v4/jobs/{id}/sbom_scans/{sbom_digest}`, but the GET operation declares a path parameter named `sbom_scan_id` instead of `sbom_digest`. The twin should reject this mismatch during route/catalog validation with a precise route refusal, or ignore the stray operation parameter and validate the actual template parameter. It should not classify the route as executable and fail at request time with a missing required field that the client could never send.

Campaign client excerpt:
```text
FAILURE GET /api/v4/jobs/{id}/sbom_scans/{sbom_digest} primary: unexpected status=422, body='{"code":"missing_required_field","detail":"required field `sbom_scan_id` is missing on resource `path`","field":"sbom_scan_id","resource":"path"}'
```

Root cause hypothesis:
`src/protocol/rest/routes.rs:1512-1535` collects all operation/path-item parameters with `in: path` into `RouteEntry.path_param_specs` but does not verify that each declared parameter name appears in `PathPattern::path_params()`. The matcher captures `sbom_digest` from the template, then `src/protocol/rest/normalize.rs:496-511` requires `sbom_scan_id` because it is present in `path_param_specs`, producing an impossible runtime 422.
notes: Fixed by CopperBasin: route registry now filters declared path parameter specs to names that appear in the parsed OpenAPI path template, so mismatched operation parameters like sbom_scan_id do not become impossible runtime requirements. Exact GitLab repro GET /api/v4/jobs/12/sbom_scans/digest-abc now returns 404 not_found instead of 422 missing_required_field for sbom_scan_id. Validation: cargo fmt --check; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test.

title: REST response stubs via x-twinning
description:
Add a generic, deterministic REST response-stub layer for twinned OpenAPI services. Motivated by OpenFIGI POST /v3/mapping: twinning can mount and validate the route and top-level array request body, but the generic mutation encoder returns {"data":null,"warning":null} because it cannot infer provider semantics from idType/idValue into a FIGI response. This feature lets OpenAPI fixtures declare exact response stubs under x-twinning so a REST twin can return known protocol-shaped payloads for selected requests while preserving normal route matching, auth enforcement, chaos injection, request logging, and existing kernel-backed behavior for non-stubbed routes.

This is not a mock-data generator, faker, semantic simulator, or stateful scenario engine. It is a fixture-backed contract response override. Callers can maintain several specs or spec overlays for the same upstream API, for example happy path, no-match, malformed response, per-item error, or ambiguity variants, and run whichever twin models the use case they need.
design:
MVP design:

- Extend top-level x-twinning parsing to accept response stubs, using either response-stubs or response_stubs as the documented spelling.
- Model a stub as id, method, path, optional request matcher, status, optional headers, and an exact JSON or text body.
- Keep matchers deliberately small for the first version: exact method/path plus optional canonical JSON body equality. Canonical JSON equality must handle object field ordering and top-level arrays. Do not add JSONPath, regex, templates, generated values, random values, or stateful scenario transitions in this bead.
- In REST dispatch, preserve the existing order up to route and safety checks: method/path route match, auth shape check, chaos injection, response-stub lookup, then existing normalize/kernel/encode fallback.
- First matching stub wins in declaration order. Startup refuses duplicate stub IDs, invalid methods, invalid statuses, unsupported matcher shapes, and stub paths that cannot be related to the mounted route table.
- Stubbed responses should still appear in the REST session report. If the report model can accept an additive marker, record that the response came from a stub and include the stub id. Otherwise keep the report schema backwards-compatible and cover the observable route/status behavior in tests.
- Keep the feature local to the REST adapter. Do not alter Postgres, MCP, Snowflake, kernel storage, seed JSONL, or snapshot behavior.
- Treat response stubs as spec fixtures. A caller can maintain several OpenAPI specs or thin spec overlays for the same upstream API, for example happy path, no-match, malformed payload, per-item error, or ambiguity twins, and run the variant that models the desired use case.
- No new CLI flag is required unless implementation proves otherwise. The runtime should consume stubs through the existing --spec path. If a CLI flag is added, it must be reflected in clap help, operator.json, README, and docs in the same change.
acceptance_criteria:
- Parser accepts top-level x-twinning.response-stubs with at least id, method, path, status, and body.
- Parser accepts the documented alias x-twinning.response_stubs only if implementation chooses to support snake_case; docs and tests must then show both or explicitly choose one spelling.
- Malformed stubs produce structured startup refusal/error output: invalid method/status, duplicate id, missing required fields, unsupported matcher shape, and paths that cannot match a known mounted route.
- Stub lookup can match mounted OpenAPI server-variable paths such as /v3/mapping after --server-variable basePath=v3 is selected.
- Stub lookup can optionally match exact canonical JSON request bodies, including top-level arrays.
- Dispatch order is auth before stub: a request missing required credentials still receives the auth refusal instead of a stubbed success.
- Dispatch order is chaos before stub: --chaos rate_limit=1.0 still produces the configured 429/Retry-After response instead of a stubbed success.
- Dispatch order is stub before generic normalize/kernel: a matched OpenFIGI-style POST can return a top-level array response instead of the generic {"data":null,"warning":null} response.
- Non-matching requests fall through to existing behavior unchanged.
- Tests cover exact hit, body mismatch fallthrough, top-level array body matcher, auth-before-stub, chaos-before-stub, duplicate/invalid stub refusal, mounted server-variable path matching, and unchanged behavior for an existing non-stub fixture.
- README.md documents response stubs under the REST twin/runtime contract, including that stubs are declared in the OpenAPI spec and are deterministic fixture responses, not generated dummy data.
- operator.json is updated so --describe and agent/operator metadata mention REST response stubs, the x-twinning extension, and the fact that no separate seed flag is required for this feature.
- CLI/help surface is accurate: src/cli.rs clap descriptions and README command examples either show the existing --spec workflow or any new flag introduced by implementation.
- docs/OPENFIGI_V2_V3_REST_TWIN_PACKET.md documents why response stubs fix the OpenFIGI {"data":null,"warning":null} fallback and how separate spec variants can model success/no-match/error/ambiguity use cases.
- Any other docs that currently describe REST x-twinning, REST seeded/example behavior, or OpenFIGI behavior are updated consistently. Use rg over README.md, operator.json, docs, tests/fixtures, and REST examples before closing.
- Quality gate: cargo fmt --check; cargo test --workspace --no-default-features --features rest; cargo test --workspace --no-default-features --features rest,postgres; and targeted clippy or documented broader clippy status.
notes:
Likely files: crates/twinning-rest/src/xext.rs, crates/twinning-rest/src/spec.rs, crates/twinning-rest/src/listener.rs, possibly crates/twinning-rest/src/report.rs, tests/rest, tests/fixtures/rest, README.md, operator.json, src/cli.rs, docs/OPENFIGI_V2_V3_REST_TWIN_PACKET.md, and docs/PLAN_TWINNING_REST_STRATEGY.md if it discusses generated REST responses.

Example target shape:

x-twinning:
  response-stubs:
    - id: openfigi_cusip_success
      method: POST
      path: /v3/mapping
      when:
        body-json-equals:
          - idType: ID_CUSIP
            idValue: "037833100"
      status: 200
      headers:
        Content-Type: application/json
      body:
        - data:
            - figi: BBG000B9XRY4
              compositeFIGI: BBG000B9XRY4
              ticker: AAPL
              name: APPLE INC
              securityType: Common Stock
              exchCode: US

Defer partial JSON matching, query/header matchers, templates, generated values, scenario state, and fixture layering syntax to follow-up beads unless this bead explicitly reopens scope.
close_reason: Implemented deterministic REST response stubs via x-twinning with exact method/path and optional canonical JSON body matching, startup validation, auth/chaos precedence, session summary counts, docs, and tests. Verified: cargo fmt --check; cargo test --workspace --no-default-features --features rest; cargo test --workspace --no-default-features --features rest,postgres; cargo test --workspace --no-default-features --features all; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --features all -- -D warnings; ./scripts/ubs_gate.sh.

title: REST GET /v1/accounts/{account}/bank_accounts/{id} returns 200 [] for empty singleton
description:
Autonomous REST e2e follow-up found while verifying bd-3hbt against the official public Stripe OpenAPI 3.0 spec.

Spec URL:
https://raw.githubusercontent.com/stripe/openapi/master/openapi/spec3.json

Local repro setup:
```bash
curl -L --fail https://raw.githubusercontent.com/stripe/openapi/master/openapi/spec3.json -o /tmp/stripe_spec3.json
cargo run -- rest --spec /tmp/stripe_spec3.json --serve --port 18186
```

Exact curl repro:
```bash
curl -i http://127.0.0.1:18186/v1/accounts/acct_test/bank_accounts/id_test \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer sk_test_twinning"
```

Actual response after bd-3hbt removes the scalar-response refusal:
```http
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
content-type: application/json

[]
```

Expected response/output:
This is a singleton retrieve route for one external account/bank account ID. On an empty twin it should return a missing-resource `404` JSON response, not a successful empty collection. Returning `200 []` is silent wrong response shape/data for a singleton GET.

Root cause hypothesis:
The startup routing table logs this route as `GET /v1/accounts/{account}/bank_accounts/{id}` with policy `prefix-scoped` and effective resource `bank_accounts` even though the declared 200 response schema is `#/components/schemas/external_account`. The likely bug is in `src/protocol/rest/routes.rs:655-682` / `route_relationship_for_response_shape()` where topology/prefix-scoped path evidence wins over the response schema and classifies the request into a collection read path, so `src/protocol/rest/encode.rs` serializes the empty read result as `[]` with 200 instead of using singleton 404 semantics.

Priority: P1 because this is a silent wrong-data/wrong-shape success response after the route becomes executable.

title: REST GET /api/v1/namespaces returns null metadata in Kubernetes list wrapper
description:
Autonomous REST e2e campaign finding from the public Kubernetes core v1 OpenAPI 3.0.0 spec.

Spec URL:
https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/master/api/openapi-spec/v3/api__v1_openapi.json

Local repro setup:
```bash
curl -L --fail https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/master/api/openapi-spec/v3/api__v1_openapi.json -o /tmp/kubernetes_core_v1_openapi.json
cargo run -- rest --spec /tmp/kubernetes_core_v1_openapi.json --serve --port 18250
```

Exact curl repro:
```bash
curl -i http://127.0.0.1:18250/api/v1/namespaces \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer e2e'
```

Actual response:
```http
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
content-type: application/json

{"apiVersion":"","kind":"","metadata":null,"items":[]}
```

Expected response/output:
The declared 200 response is `#/components/schemas/io.k8s.api.core.v1.NamespaceList`. Its `metadata` property is `ListMeta` with `default: {}` and is not nullable. If the REST twin can materialize the list wrapper, it should either omit optional `metadata` or emit an object such as `{}`. Returning `metadata: null` is a spec-invalid successful response. If nested wrapper metadata cannot be materialized, startup should refuse the route instead of returning a partial body.

The same response-shape failure appears on 28 Kubernetes list routes, including `GET /api/v1/configmaps`, `GET /api/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/pods`, and `GET /api/v1/services`.

Campaign client excerpt:
```text
response_shape: 28
  - GET /api/v1/namespaces: response shape errors: ['$.metadata: null for non-null object'], body=b'{"apiVersion":"","kind":"","metadata":null,"items":[]}'
SUMMARY operations=242 assertions=300 failures=52
```

Root cause hypothesis:
`src/protocol/rest/routes.rs:2059-2073` records every non-payload wrapper column as a static response field. `src/protocol/rest/routes.rs:2415-2425` maps `json`/object wrapper fields such as Kubernetes `metadata` to `ResponseWrapperStaticValue::Null`, and `src/protocol/rest/encode.rs:259-263` plus `src/protocol/rest/encode.rs:308-312` unconditionally emits those static fields in successful list responses. The encoder should not synthesize `null` for non-null object wrapper fields; it should omit/default them or make the route a precise unsupported-shape refusal.

Priority: P1 because this is wrong successful response data for declared OpenAPI response fields.

comments.author: Zac
comments.text: Fix note: implemented in src/protocol/rest/routes.rs and src/protocol/rest/encode.rs. ResponseWrapperStaticValue now has object/array variants, wrapper static field derivation preserves declared object/array shapes, and the encoder emits {} / [] instead of null for those fields. Repro GET /api/v1/namespaces now returns metadata:{}; Kubernetes client response_shape failures dropped from 28 to 0. Full gate passed: cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test.

title: Prove embedded verify over child-materialized run_once state
description:
Rationale:
The embedded verify bridge exists, but the end-to-end run_once path must prove it evaluates the committed state produced by the child, not an empty or stale table set.

Acceptance Criteria:
- A child-created violation produces outcome FAIL with attached verify.report.v1 and process exit 1.
- A child-created clean state produces PASS or READY according to the existing report outcome rules.
- No batch verify shell-out path is introduced.
close_reason: Added black-box run_once verify proof over child-committed state
